description: Fix all broken modules before building new features. Every story must work on a real Android device with real data flowing to Supabase before marked complete.

userStories.title: Fix AndroidManifest Health Connect permissions
acceptanceCriteria: android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml contains all 8 health READ permissions inside <manifest> tag
android/app/src/main/res/values/health_permissions.xml created with all 7 health permission items
Intent filter for androidx.health.ACTION_SHOW_PERMISSIONS_RATIONALE added inside <activity> tag
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
App builds and runs without crash on Android device
userStories.technicalNotes: File: android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml. Permissions needed: READ_SLEEP, READ_STEPS, READ_HEART_RATE, READ_WEIGHT, READ_EXERCISE, READ_DISTANCE, READ_ACTIVE_CALORIES_BURNED, READ_BODY_FAT. health_service.dart is already complete — this is purely a manifest fix.

userStories.title: Register WorkManager background health sync
acceptanceCriteria: WorkManager callback registered in lib/main.dart before runApp()
Background task calls HealthService.syncHealthData() then HealthRepositoryImpl.saveHealthMetrics()
Sync interval set to minimum 15 minutes (WorkManager constraint)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
After closing app and waiting 15 minutes, new rows appear in wt_health_metrics table in Supabase
userStories.technicalNotes: File: lib/main.dart and lib/features/health/data/health_background_sync.dart. The sync class already exists — it just needs wiring to WorkManager in main.dart. Use workmanager package already in pubspec.

userStories.title: Fix notification service so reminders fire on device
acceptanceCriteria: flutter_local_notifications initialised in lib/main.dart before runApp()
POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission requested on Android 13+ on first app launch
AndroidManifest.xml includes POST_NOTIFICATIONS, SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM, RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED permissions
Boot receiver registered so reminders reschedule after device restart
Every reminder saved to wt_reminders table also schedules a device notification via notification_service.scheduleNotification()
Tapping a notification deep links to the correct module screen
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: create a reminder set 2 minutes from now — device notification fires at correct time
userStories.technicalNotes: File: lib/features/reminders/data/notification_service.dart and lib/main.dart. The reminder creation UI works. The notification_service.dart exists but is not initialised or called. Use flutter_local_notifications package already in pubspec.

userStories.title: Integrate Open Food Facts food database API
acceptanceCriteria: lib/features/meals/data/food_database_service.dart created
Keyword search calls https://world.openfoodfacts.org/cgi/search.pl and returns name, calories per 100g, protein, carbs, fat, image_url
Barcode search calls https://world.openfoodfacts.org/api/v0/product/{barcode}.json and returns same fields
Results cached in Hive to reduce repeat API calls (key = search term or barcode)
Existing barcode scanner (mobile_scanner) connected to barcode search method
Food search screen allows keyword search with results list showing name, calories, macro summary
Selecting a food item returns it to the meal log flow
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: search 'chicken breast' returns results with calorie and macro data
userStories.technicalNotes: This is the most critical gap — without a food database, users cannot log real food. Open Food Facts is free with no API key required. Use Dio (already installed) for HTTP calls. Cache with Hive (already installed). Connect to existing barcode scanner widget.

userStories.title: Fix meals module user interactions
acceptanceCriteria: Mark planned meal as eaten — one tap updates meal status and refreshes macro progress bar
Mark eaten with portion adjustment — user can select 50%, 75%, 100%, 125%, 150% or enter custom grams
Log food not in plan — triggers food database search (story 3b-4), logs result against today
Macro progress bar on meals screen updates in real time as meals are marked eaten
Swap meal button shows 3 macro-matched AI alternatives — user selects one, plan updates
Regenerate single meal slot — replaces one meal without affecting rest of plan
Calorie target on meal plan screen reads from wt_profiles (not hardcoded)
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: mark breakfast as eaten — macro bar updates, daily total changes correctly
userStories.technicalNotes: File: lib/features/meals/presentation/. The meal plan generation works. The missing piece is user interaction — tap handlers, portion logic, and swap functionality. Swap should call ai_orchestrator_service.dart with current meal macros and ask for 3 alternatives.

userStories.title: Fix shopping list ingredient generation
acceptanceCriteria: Shopping list auto-generates from current active meal plan by extracting all ingredients with quantities
Quantities aggregated correctly across all meals in the plan (e.g. 3 meals using chicken = total grams combined)
Items cross-referenced with pantry_repository — items already in pantry excluded from list
Items grouped by category: produce, dairy, meat/fish, frozen, dry goods, other
Each item has a tick-off checkbox — tapping marks it bought
Manual add item input works for non-plan items
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: generate list from a 7-day meal plan — list shows categorised ingredients excluding pantry items
userStories.technicalNotes: File: lib/features/shopping/data/ and lib/features/shopping/presentation/. The shopping list screen exists but ingredient extraction from meal plans is broken. shopping_list_repository.dart needs to query current meal plan → extract ingredients → aggregate → cross-ref pantry.

userStories.title: Connect goals projection chart to real health data
acceptanceCriteria: Goal projection chart reads real metric data from wt_health_metrics via health_repository_impl.dart
Weight goal projection calculates trend from actual logged weight entries
VO2 max trend line shows actual data points from Health Connect workouts
Projected completion date calculated from weighted moving average (14-28 day window, 2x recent bias)
Warning displayed when projected date exceeds goal deadline
Chart shows at minimum 7 days of data points before rendering trend line
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: log 7 days of weight data — projection chart shows trend line with estimated goal date
userStories.technicalNotes: File: lib/features/goals/presentation/widgets/goal_projection_chart.dart. The chart widget exists with mock data. Replace mock data with real Supabase queries via the existing goal_repository.dart and health_repository_impl.dart.

userStories.title: Connect performance and prescription engines to real data
acceptanceCriteria: performance_engine.dart calculates recovery score from real wt_health_metrics rows (not stub data)
Recovery score formula: sleep_score×0.30 + sleep_quality×0.20 + rhr_score×0.25 + load_score×0.25
RHR score uses personal 14-day baseline from real logged data
prescription_engine.dart reads recovery score and outputs correct plan type (push/normal/easy/rest)
Plan type correctly sets workout recommendation and calorie adjustment
Recovery score displays on insights dashboard with today's value and 7-day sparkline
If fewer than 14 days of data exist, dashboard shows 'Collecting your baseline — Day X of 14'
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: with real Health Connect data synced, recovery score shows a non-null value on dashboard
userStories.technicalNotes: Files: lib/features/insights/data/performance_engine.dart and lib/features/daily_coach/data/prescription_engine.dart. Engines are built but query stub/hardcoded data. Wire them to health_repository_impl.dart to pull real metrics. Recovery score is the product's core differentiator — get this right.

userStories.title: Fix workout live session rest timer and overload detection
acceptanceCriteria: Rest timer auto-starts immediately after each set is logged
Rest timer shows countdown on screen with vibration + sound alert when period ends
1RM auto-calculates from logged sets using Epley formula: weight × (1 + reps/30)
Current 1RM displays live on exercise header during session
If today's calculated 1RM exceeds stored personal best, PR celebration overlay shown
Session summary screen shows: total volume (kg), duration, PRs hit, muscle groups trained
overload_detection_service.dart output shown as suggestion card on session summary
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Test: log 3 sets of bench press — rest timer fires, 1RM updates, summary shows correct volume
userStories.technicalNotes: File: lib/features/workouts/presentation/workout_logging_screen.dart. The screen exists with set logging. rest_timer_provider.dart exists but is not auto-triggering. overload_detection_service.dart exists but output not surfaced in UI. Use vibration package already installed.
